id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

preferences.description: User's preferences as a json string.

wellnessGoals.description: A string containing the user's wellness goals.

JournalEntry.title: JournalEntry
JournalEntry.description: Represents a journal entry for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JournalEntry entity.

CommunityPost.title: CommunityPost
CommunityPost.description: Represents a post in the community forum.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CommunityPost entity.

authorName.description: The name of the post author.

ChatbotSession.title: ChatbotSession
ChatbotSession.description: Represents a chat session between a user and the chatbot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatbotSession entity.

startTime.description: Start time of the chat session.

endTime.description: End time of the chat session.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a single message within a chat session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Parent document for user-owned subcollections.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores journal entries for a specific user.  Uses path-based ownership for security.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: journalEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the journal entry.

definition.description: Stores chatbot sessions for a specific user. Uses path-based ownership for security.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: chatbotSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chatbot session.

definition.description: Stores chat messages within a chatbot session for a specific user.  Uses path-based ownership for security.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: chatbotSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chatbot session.

params.name: chatMessageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat message.

definition.description: Stores community posts. All users can read posts. Only the owner can update or delete. The `userId` field indicates the owner.

params.name: communityPostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the community post.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, while adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). 

To achieve Authorization Independence, user-specific data such as Journal Entries, and Chatbot Sessions are stored as subcollections under the `/users/{userId}` path. This denormalizes the user ID, enabling security rules to directly verify ownership without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. User wellness goals are stored on the UserProfile document for simplicity. Community Posts are stored in a separate collection, allowing for more flexible access control and QAPs related to public content.

The structure facilitates secure `list` operations by segregating data with different access requirements into distinct collections (e.g., user-specific data vs. community posts).  Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) is used for private data, ensuring that only the authenticated user can access their own data. This approach simplifies security rules and enhances maintainability.

Predictable schema and semantic naming conventions (e.g., `userId`, `postDate`) are used throughout the structure to enhance clarity and consistency.

Overall, this design promotes a clear separation of concerns, simplifies security rule implementation, and supports scalable data management.
